joke: I told my suitcase there’d be no vacations this year. Now I’m dealing with emotional baggage.
Why don’t skeletons fight each other? They don’t have the guts.
Why was the math book sad? It had too many problems.
Why did the tomato blush? Because it saw the salad dressing.
I tried to catch some fog yesterday. Mist.
I asked my dog what’s two minus two. He said nothing.
I’m reading a book about anti-gravity. It’s impossible to put down.
My keyboard and I broke up. It said I wasn’t its type.
I used to play piano by ear, but now I use my hands.
Why did the calendar look nervous? Its days were numbered.
My boss told me to have a good day, so I went home.
What did one wall say to the other? “I’ll meet you at the corner.”
Why did the painting get arrested? It was framed.
I used to be afraid of hurdles, but I got over it.
What did the ocean say to the beach? Nothing—it just waved.
The shovel was a groundbreaking invention.
Why are balloons so expensive? Inflation.
Why did the belt get promoted? It always held everything together.
Why can’t you trust stairs? They’re always up to something.
I entered ten puns in a contest, hoping one would win. No pun in ten did.
A burglar broke into my house looking for money, so I got up and helped him.
My mirror and I have a lot in common—we both look better in the dark.
I’m great at multitasking—I can procrastinate, worry, and do nothing all at once.
I tried organizing a hide-and-seek tournament, but good players are hard to find.
Two antennas got married. The ceremony was dull, but the reception was excellent.
I told a chemistry joke, but there was no reaction.
Why did the scarecrow win an award? It was outstanding in its field.
Why did the soap refuse to argue? It wanted to come clean.
Why did the light bulb fail its exam? It wasn’t very bright.
Why did the bicycle fall over? It was two-tired.
